attestation.statement: Remote attestation not configured; local no-op evidence recorded.
Remote attestation not configured; local no-op evidence recorded.

proposal.research_question: How do out-of-pocket spending and government health expenditure affect catastrophic health expenditure across countries?
proposal.methodology: Panel data regression with Fixed Effects (two-way) and clustered standard errors
robustness_checks: Clustered standard errors at country level
proposal.task_required_estimator_justification: Fixed effects controls for time-invariant country heterogeneity

violations.message: An RCT workflow must define treatment arm(s) and the control/comparison group.
violations.guidance: List all treatment arms and name the reference/control condition.

violations.rule_name: RCT Requires Assignment Probability or Randomization Method
violations.message: RCT workflows should document the assignment probability or randomization method.
violations.guidance: Record treatment ratios, lottery/permutation/stratified assignment, or the exact randomization procedure.
violations.citation: J-PAL, Randomization methods and assignment shares

violations.rule_name: RCT Reproducibility Requires Seed or Assignment File
violations.message: Software-generated randomization should record a seed or archived assignment file.
violations.guidance: Store the seed, assignment script, or final assignment file so the randomization can be audited.

violations.message: RCT workflows should report baseline balance across treatment arms.
violations.guidance: Report balance on key pre-treatment covariates and any stratification variables.
violations.citation: J-PAL; Bruhn, Miriam; McKenzie, David, Randomization and balance

violations.message: RCT workflows should check attrition overall and by treatment arm.
violations.guidance: Report attrition rates and use bounds or sensitivity analysis when attrition may be differential.
violations.citation: J-PAL, Attrition, bounds, and inverse-probability weighting

violations.rule_name: RCT Requires Spillover or Interference Plan
violations.message: RCT workflows should document spillover/interference risks.
violations.guidance: Explain why interference is unlikely, or specify spillover-aware measurement, clustering, or sensitivity checks.
violations.citation: J-PAL, Internal validity, spillovers, and SUTVA

violations.message: RCT workflows should document power calculations or minimum detectable effects.
violations.guidance: Add the power calculation, assignment shares, clustering assumptions, and minimum detectable effect.

violations.rule_name: RCT Should Register Trial and PAP
violations.message: RCT workflows should document trial registration and a pre-analysis plan when available.
violations.guidance: Register or link the trial and PAP, or document why registration is unavailable.
violations.citation: J-PAL, Trial registration and pre-analysis plans

violations.message: RCT workflows should explain why interference or multiple treatment versions do not undermine the estimand.
violations.guidance: Document the SUTVA rationale in plain research language, especially when treatment is delivered through networks, classrooms, clinics, villages, or markets.

violations.rule_name: Panel FE Requires Unit and Time Index
violations.message: Panel workflows must declare both the unit index and time index.
violations.guidance: Record the panel unit and time variables before estimating fixed, random, or pooled panel models.
violations.citation: Wooldridge, Jeffrey M.; Verbeek, Marno, Panel data structure

violations.message: Fixed-effects workflows should explain the remaining within-unit identifying variation.
violations.guidance: Describe the within-unit changes that identify the coefficient after fixed effects.
violations.citation: Wooldridge, Jeffrey M.; Verbeek, Marno, Within-estimation interpretation
